description: Fires when the hash or browser history changes, with a delay (e.g. wait 1 second) before firing. Designed for single-page applications (SPAs): the delay helps match page URLs with page titles and avoids recording multiple rapid navigations as separate page views. If multiple history changes occur during the delay, only the last one is recorded. Events before the delay are attributed to the previous page. Recommended over the plain history trigger for more accurate SPA tracking. Same conditions as History change trigger (Page URL, Page path, History source, History fragment, etc.). See Piwik PRO help: History change trigger.

relationship_to_history_trigger.note: Two history trigger types exist in the API: 'history' and 'debounced_history'. Use 'history' to fire on every hash/history change. Use 'debounced_history' when you want the 'Wait 1 second before firing' behaviour for better SPA accuracy.
relationship_to_history_trigger.same_conditions: Condition types and structure are the same as for the History change trigger.

condition_types.description: Fires when a history or hash change (after delay) occurs on the selected page URL
Fires when a history or hash change occurs on a page with the selected path
Fires when a history or hash change occurs on the selected hostname
Fires when a specific history event occurs (pushState, replaceState, popState or hashchange)
Fires when the URL hash matches the selected condition
Fires when the previous URL hash matches
Fires when the history state matches
condition_types.example: History state is set or History state equals my-state

condition_types.description: Fires when the previous history state matches
Fires when a history/hash change occurs on a page from the selected internal referrer
Fires when a history/hash change occurs on a page from the selected external referrer
Fires when a history/hash change occurs on a page from the selected campaign
Fires when the visitor is a returning visitor and a history or hash change occurs
Any variable can be used to define trigger conditions

when_to_use: Track SPA virtual page views with accurate page URL and title matching
Avoid duplicate or out-of-order events when users navigate quickly in an SPA
Attribute events to the correct virtual page after the delay (recommended for SPAs)
common_use_cases: SPA route change tracking with 1-second wait for stable page title
Virtual page views where only the last navigation in a burst is recorded
React/Vue/Angular apps where history changes are frequent and rapid
best_practices: Prefer debounced_history over history for SPAs so data is more accurate
When the wait is on: tag fires only if the visitor stays on the page at least one second; events before the delay are attributed to the previous page
Add conditions (Page path, History fragment, etc.) to restrict when the tag fires

create_trigger.description: Create a debounced history change trigger. Fires after a short delay when hash or history changes; recommended for SPAs.

trigger_type.description: Must be 'debounced_history' for debounced history change triggers

conditions.description: Array of condition objects; same as history trigger. Optional.

update_trigger.description: Update an existing debounced history change trigger – only editable fields will be processed

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Same as history trigger (Page URL, Page path, History source, History fragment, Referrer, etc.). Optional.
conditions.recommendation: Use conditions to fire only on relevant SPA routes or history events.

spa_debounced_example.description: Debounced history trigger for SPA (fires after delay; only last change in a burst is recorded)

debounced_with_condition_example.description: Debounced history only when History fragment equals pricing

common_mistakes: Using debounced_history on non-SPA sites where page view is sufficient
Expecting the tag to fire immediately – it fires after the configured delay
Assuming every history change is recorded – only the last change in the delay window is recorded

possible_causes: Visitor navigates away before the delay ends
SPA does not use hash or History API
debugging_steps: Confirm history/hash changes occur in the app
Ensure visitor stays on the new 'page' at least until the delay completes
